description_long: This object is a detailed model of a crustacean resembling a stag beetle or a similar arthropod, featuring a robust, segmented exoskeleton with a muted brownish-gray coloration. The body is elongated, with a pronounced cephalothorax and a slightly tapered abdomen. It has six long, spindly legs, each jointed and ending in fine points, giving it a delicate yet menacing appearance. The most striking feature is the pair of large, curved pincers or mandibles extending from the head, which are prominent and appear capable of grasping or pinching. The surface of the exoskeleton is hard and slightly matte, with subtle textural details suggesting a chitinous composition. Small, subtle eyes are positioned on the head, and short antennae are visible, adding to its lifelike realism.
description: A realistic, brownish-gray crustacean with six spindly legs and large, curved pincers, featuring a segmented, hard exoskeleton and subtle surface texture.
description_view_0: Side view highlighting the elongated body, large curved pincers, and the full length of the spindly legs.
description_view_1: Top-down view showing the symmetrical arrangement of legs, the segmentation of the body, and the width of the cephalothorax.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the curvature of the pincers and the tapering of the abdomen.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the imposing pincers, the head structure, and the forward orientation of the legs.
